sections.label: Section 675
sections.original: στείχοντα δ αὐτόφορτον οἰκείᾳ σαγῇ [*]
sections.translation: just as I ended my journey here , [*] a man , a stranger to me as I to him , fell in with me , and inquired about my destination and told me his . He was Strophius , a Phocian ( for as we talked I learned his name ), and he said to me ,

sections.label: Section 680
sections.original: “ ἐπείπερ ἄλλως , ὦ ξέν , εἰς Ἄργος κίεις ,
sections.translation: Stranger , since in any case you are bound for Argos , keep my message in mind most faithfully and tell his parents Orestes is dead , and by no means let it escape you . Whether his friends decide to bring him home or to bury him in the land of his sojourn , a foreigner utterly forever ,

sections.label: Section 685
sections.original: θάπτειν , ἐφετμὰς τάσδε πόρθμευσον πάλιν .
sections.translation: convey their wishes back to me . In the meantime a bronze urn contains the ashes of a man rightly lamented . This much I tell you as I heard it . Whether by any chance I am speaking to those with whom the question rests and whose concern it is ,

sections.label: Section 690
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδα , τὸν τεκόντα δ εἰκὸς εἰδέναι .
sections.translation: I do not know . But his parent should know the truth .

sections.label: Section 691
sections.original: οἲ ʼγώ , κατ ἄκρας εἶπας [*] ὡς πορθούμεθα .
sections.translation: Oh no ! Your story spells our utter undoing . O curse that haunts this house , so hard to wrestle down : how far forward you look ! Even what was laid well out of harm ’ s way you bring down with your well-aimed shafts from far off ,

sections.label: Section 695
sections.original: φίλων ἀποψιλοῖς με τὴν παναθλίαν .
sections.translation: and you strip me of those I love , utterly wretched as I am . And now Orestes : he was indeed prudent in keeping his foot out of the mire of destruction , but now mark down as having abandoned us what was once the one hope in our house of a cure for its fine revelry . [*]

sections.label: Section 700
sections.original: ἐγὼ μὲν οὖν ξένοισιν ὧδ εὐδαίμοσιν
sections.translation: As for me , I am sure that with hosts so prosperous I would rather have been made known and welcomed for favorable news . For where is goodwill greater than from guest to host ? Yet to my mind it would have been irreverent

sections.label: Section 705
sections.original: τοιόνδε πρᾶγμα μὴ καρανῶσαι φίλοις ,
sections.translation: not to fulfill for friends a charge like this when I was bound by promise and hospitality pledged to me .

sections.label: Section 707
sections.original: οὔτοι κυρήσεις μεῖον ἀξίων [*] σέθεν ,
sections.translation: Rest assured you will receive no less a reward than you deserve nor be the less welcome to this house : someone else might just as well have brought your message .

sections.label: Section 710
sections.original: ἀλλ ἔσθ ὁ καιρὸς ἡμερεύοντας ξένους
sections.translation: But it is the hour when strangers who have been travelling on a long day ’ s journey should have their proper entertainment . To an attendant Conduct him to the rooms where the men are hospitably lodged , him and his attendants here and his fellow-traveller , and let them be tended to there as is proper in our house .

sections.label: Section 715
sections.original: αἰνῶ δὲ πράσσειν ὡς ὑπευθύνῳ [*] τάδε .
sections.translation: I command you to do this as you shall be held to strict account . Meantime we will communicate this matter to the master of the house , and since we have no lack of friends we will confer on this occurrence .

sections.label: Section 719
sections.original: εἶεν , φίλιαι δμωίδες οἴκων ,
sections.translation: Ah , loyal handmaidens of the house ,

sections.label: Section 720
sections.original: πότε δὴ στομάτων
sections.translation:
low long will it be before we display the power that lies in our mouths to do Orestes service ?
O hallowed earth , and hallowed barrow raised high that now lies on the royal form of the commander of the fleet ,

sections.label: Section 725
sections.original: νῦν ἐπάκουσον , νῦν ἐπάρηξον :
sections.translation: now hear me , now lend me aid ! Now is the hour for Persuasion with her guile to join forces with him , and for Hermes of the nether world , who works in stealth , to direct this encounter of the deadly sword . Enter Orestes ’ Nurse

sections.label: Section 730
sections.original: ἔοικεν ἁνὴρ [*] ὁ ξένος τεύχειν κακόν :
sections.translation: Our stranger , I think , is working mischief : for over there I see Orestes ’ nurse all in tears . Cilissa [*] ! Where are you going ? Why as you set foot in the palace gate do you have a grief as an unhired companion ?

sections.label: Section 734
sections.original: Αἴγισθον ἡ κρατοῦσα τοῖς ξένοις [*] καλεῖν
sections.translation: My mistress commands me to summon Aegisthus for the strangers

sections.label: Section 735
sections.original: ὅπως τάχιστ ἄνωγεν , ὡς σαφέστερον
sections.translation: in all haste , so that he may come and learn more clearly , from man to man , these tidings that have just arrived . Indeed , before the servants , behind eyes that feigned grief she hid her laughter over what has occurred fortunately for her .

sections.label: Section 740
sections.original: κείνῃ , [*] δόμοις δὲ τοῖσδε παγκάκως ἔχειν ,
sections.translation: But the news so plainly told by the strangers means utter ruin for this house . I expect that when he hears it he will rejoice in his heart to know the story . Miserable woman that I am ! How the old unbearable troubles of every sort

sections.label: Section 745
sections.original: ἄλγη δύσοιστα τοῖσδ ἐν Ἀτρέως δόμοις
sections.translation: that occurred in this house of Atreus have always made my heart ache within my breast ! But never yet have I endured a blow like this . For all the other troubles I bore patiently , but my beloved Orestes , on whom I spent my soul ,

sections.label: Section 750
sections.original: ὃν ἐξέθρεψα μητρόθεν δεδεγμένη ,—
sections.translation: whom I took from his mother at birth and nursed , and the many and troublesome tasks , fruitless for all my enduring them , when his loud and urgent cries broke my rest .... For one must nurse the senseless thing like a dumb beast , of course one must , by following its humor .

sections.label: Section 755
sections.original: οὐ γάρ τι φωνεῖ παῖς ἔτ ὢν ἐν σπαργάνοις ,
sections.translation: For while it is still a baby in swaddling clothes , it has no speech at all , whether hunger moves it , or thirst perhaps , or the call of need : children ’ s young insides work their own relief . I would anticipate these needs . Yet many a time , I think , having to wash the child ’ s linen because of my own errors ,

sections.label: Section 760
sections.original: γναφεὺς τροφεύς [*] τε ταὐτὸν εἰχέτην τέλος .
sections.translation: laundress and nurse had the same function . It was I who , with these two handicrafts , received Orestes for his father . And now , wretch that I am , I hear that he is dead . But I am on my way to fetch the man who wrought destruction on

sections.label: Section 765
sections.original: οἴκων , θέλων δὲ τόνδε πεύσεται λόγον . [*]
sections.translation: our house , and he will be glad enough to hear this news .

sections.label: Section 766
sections.original: πῶς οὖν κελεύει νιν μολεῖν ἐσταλμένον ;
sections.translation: Then arrayed how does she tell him to come ?

sections.label: Section 767
sections.original: ἦ πῶς ; [*] λέγ αὖθις , ὡς μάθω σαφέστερον .
sections.translation: Arrayed how ? Say it again so that I may catch your meaning better .

sections.label: Section 768
sections.original: εἰ [*] ξὺν λοχίταις εἴτε καὶ μονοστιβῆ .
sections.translation: With his guards or perhaps unattended ?

sections.label: Section 769
sections.original: ἄγειν κελεύει δορυφόρους ὀπάονας .
sections.translation: She tells him to come with his retinue of spearmen .

sections.label: Section 770
sections.original: μή νυν σὺ ταῦτ ἄγγελλε δεσπότου στύγει :
sections.translation: Well , do not give this message to our loathed master , but with all haste and with a cheerful heart tell him to come himself , alone , so that he may be told without alarm . For in the mouth of a messenger a crooked message is made straight . [*]

sections.label: Section 774
sections.original: ἀλλ ἦ φρονεῖς εὖ τοῖσι νῦν ἠγγελμένοις ;
sections.translation: What ! Are you gladdened at heart by the present news ?

sections.label: Section 775
sections.original: ἀλλ εἰ τροπαίαν Ζεὺς κακῶν θήσει ποτέ .
sections.translation: Why not , if Zeus at last may cause our ill wind to change ?

sections.label: Section 776
sections.original: καὶ πῶς ; Ὀρέστης ἐλπὶς οἴχεται δόμων .
sections.translation: But how can that be ? Orestes , the hope of our house , is gone .

sections.label: Section 777
sections.original: οὔπω : κακός γε μάντις ἂν γνοίη τάδε .
sections.translation: Not yet ; he would be a poor prophet who would so interpret .

sections.label: Section 778
sections.original: τί φής ; ἔχεις τι τῶν λελεγμένων δίχα ;
sections.translation: What are you saying ? Do you know something beyond what has been told ?

sections.label: Section 779
sections.original: ἄγγελλ [*] ἰοῦσα , πρᾶσσε τἀπεσταλμένα .
sections.translation: Go , deliver your message ! Do what you are asked to do !

sections.label: Section 780
sections.original: μέλει θεοῖσιν ὧνπερ ἂν μέλῃ [*] πέρι .
sections.translation: The gods take care of what they take care of .

sections.label: Section 781
sections.original: ἀλλ εἶμι καὶ σοῖς ταῦτα πείσομαι λόγοις .
sections.translation: Well , I will go and do your bidding . With the gods ’ blessing may everything turn out for the best ! Exit

sections.label: Section 1.783
sections.original: νῦν παραιτουμένᾳ μοι , [*] πάτερ
sections.translation: Now at my supplication , O Zeus , father of the Olympian gods , grant that the fortunes of our house be firmly established ,

sections.label: Section 1.785
sections.original: δὸς τύχας εὖ τυχεῖν [*] κυρίως
sections.translation: so that those who rightly desire the rule of order may behold it . Every word of mine has been uttered in justice . O Zeus , may you safeguard it !

sections.label: Section 1.789
sections.original: ἐέ , πρὸ δὲ δὴ χθρῶν
sections.translation: O Zeus , set him who is within the palace before his foes ;

sections.label: Section 1.790
sections.original: τὸν ἔσωθεν μελάθρων , Ζεῦ , [*]
sections.translation: since , if you exalt him , he will gladly pay you with double and triple recompense .

sections.label: Section 1.794
sections.original: ἴσθι [*] δ ἀνδρὸς φίλου πῶλον εὖ-
sections.translation: Know that the orphaned colt of a loved one is harnessed to the chariot of distress .

sections.label: Section 1.795
sections.original: νιν ζυγέντ ἐν ἅρμασιν [*]
sections.translation: And by setting bounds to his course may you grant that we see him keep a steady pace through this race and win the goal in the straining stride of a gallop . [*]

sections.label: Section 2.800
sections.original: οἵ τ ἔσω [*] δωμάτων
sections.translation: And you who within the house inhabit the inner chamber that exults in its wealth , hear me , you gods , that feel with us ! By a fresh award redeem the blood of deeds done long ago .

sections.label: Section 2.805
sections.original: γέρων φόνος μηκέτ ἐν δόμοις τέκοι .
sections.translation: May aged Murder cease begetting offspring in our house !

sections.label: Section 2.806
sections.original: τὸ δὲ [*] καλῶς κτίμενον [*] ὦ μέγα ναίων
sections.translation: And you who occupy the mighty , gorgeously built cavern , [*] grant that the man ’ s house may lift up its eyes again in joy , and that with glad

sections.label: Section 2.810
sections.original: ὄμμασιν [*] ἐκ [*] δνοφερᾶς καλύπτρας .
sections.translation: eyes it may behold from under its veil of gloom the radiant light of freedom .

sections.label: Section 2.811
sections.original: ξυλλάβοι δ ἐνδίκως
sections.translation: May Maia ’ s son , [*] as he rightfully should , lend his aid , for no one can better sail a deed on a favoring course , when he would do so . [*]

sections.label: Section 2.815
sections.original: [ πολλὰ δ ἄλλα φανεῖ χρηίζων κρυπτά [*] ].
sections.translation: But by his mysterious utterance he brings darkness over men ’ s eyes by night , and by day he is no more clear at all .

sections.label: Section 3.819
sections.original: καὶ τότ [*] ἤδη κλυτὸν [*]
sections.translation: And then at last with a loud voice we shall sing a song of the deliverance of our house ,

sections.label: Section 3.820
sections.original: δωμάτων λυτήριον ,
sections.translation: the song that women raise when the wind sits fair , and not the shrill strain of those who mourn : The ship goes well . This grows to profit for me , for me , and calamity holds off from those I love .

sections.label: Section 3.827
sections.original: σὺ δὲ θαρσῶν , ὅταν ἥκῃ μέρος ἔργων ,
sections.translation: But may you with good courage , when the part of action comes , cry out loud the name Father when she exclaims Son ,

sections.label: Section 3.830
sections.original: [ καὶ ] πέραιν [*] ἀνεπίμομφον ἄταν .
sections.translation: and accomplish the baneful but irreproachable deed .

sections.label: Section 3.831
sections.original: Περσέως τ ἐν φρεσὶν
sections.translation: Raise up Perseus ’ spirit within my breast . And for those dear to you below the earth , and for those above , exact satisfaction for their dire wrath

sections.label: Section 3.835
sections.original: χάριτας [*] ὀργᾶς λυγρᾶς , [*] ἔνδοθεν
sections.translation: by working bloody ruin in our house and obliterating the guilt of murder . [*]

sections.label: Section 838
sections.original: ἥκω μὲν οὐκ ἄκλητος , ἀλλ ὑπάγγελος :
sections.translation: I have come not unasked but summoned by a messenger . I heard startling news told by some strangers who have arrived , tidings far from welcome :

sections.label: Section 840
sections.original: ξένους μολόντας οὐδαμῶς ἐφίμερον , [*]
sections.translation: — that Orestes is dead . To lay this too upon our house would be a fearful burden when it is still festering and galled by the wound inflicted by an earlier murder . How can I believe this tale is the living truth ? Or is it merely a panic-stricken report spread by women

sections.label: Section 845
sections.original: ἢ πρὸς γυναικῶν δειματούμενοι λόγοι
sections.translation: which leaps up to die away in nothingness ? What can you tell me of this to make it plain to my mind ?

sections.label: Section 848
sections.original: ἠκούσαμεν μέν , πυνθάνου δὲ τῶν ξένων
sections.translation: We heard the tale , it is true . But go inside and inquire of the strangers . The certainty of a messenger ’ s report

sections.label: Section 850
sections.original: ὡς αὐτὸν [*] αὐτῶν ἄνδρα πεύθεσθαι πάρα . [*]
sections.translation: is nothing compared with one ’ s own interrogation of the man himself .

sections.label: Section 851
sections.original: ἰδεῖν ἐλέγξαι τ αὖ θέλω τὸν ἄγγελον ,
sections.translation: I wish to see the messenger and put him to the test again — whether he himself was present at the death or merely repeats from vague reports what he has heard . No ! Be sure he cannot deceive a mind with eyes open . Exit
